questions.question: Which is a Type 1 (bare-metal) hypervisor?
questions.explanation: ESXi runs directly on hardware without a host OS underneath.

correct.explanation: ESXi runs directly on hardware without a host OS underneath.

incorrect.explanation: VMware Workstation on Windows misses what this question tests. ESXi runs directly on hardware without a host OS underneath.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "VMware Workstation on Windows" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Docker Desktop only misses what this question tests. ESXi runs directly on hardware without a host OS underneath.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Docker Desktop only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: A physical router reflects a common trap: Virtualization eliminates physical networks. VMs/containers still need virtual and physical switching/routing underneath.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Virtualization eliminates physical networks

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Virtualization eliminates physical networks — VMs/containers still need virtual and physical switching/routing underneath

questions.question: A Type 2 hypervisor runs…
choices: On top of a host operating system
questions.explanation: Type 2 is hosted — e.g. Workstation on Windows/macOS.

correct.explanation: Type 2 is hosted — e.g. Workstation on Windows/macOS.

incorrect.explanation: Directly on bare metal only misses what this question tests. Type 2 is hosted — e.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Directly on bare metal only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Inside a container only misses what this question tests. Type 2 is hosted — e.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Inside a container only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Without any VMs misses what this question tests. Type 2 is hosted — e.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Without any VMs" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Virtualization eliminates physical networks — VMs/containers still need virtual and physical switching/routing underneath

questions.question: Containers differ from VMs because containers…
choices: Include a full guest OS each
questions.explanation: Containers isolate processes but share one kernel — lighter than full VMs.

correct.explanation: Containers isolate processes but share one kernel — lighter than full VMs.

incorrect.explanation: Include a full guest OS each reflects a common trap: Saying containers include a full guest OS. Containers share the host kernel — only apps/libs are packaged.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Saying containers include a full guest OS

incorrect.explanation: Cannot run on Linux misses what this question tests. Containers isolate processes but share one kernel — lighter than full VMs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Cannot run on Linux" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Replace physical switches misses what this question tests. Containers isolate processes but share one kernel — lighter than full VMs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Replace physical switches" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Saying containers include a full guest OS — Containers share the host kernel — only apps/libs are packaged

questions.question: VRF on a router provides…
questions.explanation: VRF creates separate routing/forwarding instances on one device.

correct.explanation: VRF creates separate routing/forwarding instances on one device.

incorrect.explanation: Wireless encryption misses what this question tests. VRF creates separate routing/forwarding instances on one device.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Wireless encryption" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Fiber transceiver support misses what this question tests. VRF creates separate routing/forwarding instances on one device.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Fiber transceiver support" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: DHCP server redundancy misses what this question tests. VRF creates separate routing/forwarding instances on one device.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "DHCP server redundancy" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Equating VRF with VLAN — VRF isolates L3 routing; VLAN isolates L2 broadcast domains

questions.question: NFV (Network Function Virtualization) means…
choices: Running network services as software on general hardware
questions.explanation: NFV virtualizes functions like firewall and router as software.

correct.explanation: NFV virtualizes functions like firewall and router as software.

incorrect.explanation: Replacing DNS with VLANs misses what this question tests. NFV virtualizes functions like firewall and router as software.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Replacing DNS with VLANs" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Eliminating all routers misses what this question tests. NFV virtualizes functions like firewall and router as software.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Eliminating all routers" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Using only WEP misses what this question tests. NFV virtualizes functions like firewall and router as software.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Using only WEP" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: NFV and VMs are the same thing — NFV is a use case — virtualized network functions; VMs are one way to host them (containers another)

questions.question: Each virtual machine typically includes…
choices: Its own guest OS and virtual NICs
Shared kernel with all other VMs
questions.explanation: VMs are full guests with virtual hardware including vNICs.

correct.explanation: VMs are full guests with virtual hardware including vNICs.

incorrect.explanation: Only a container image misses what this question tests. VMs are full guests with virtual hardware including vNICs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Only a container image" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Shared kernel with all other VMs misses what this question tests. VMs are full guests with virtual hardware including vNICs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Shared kernel with all other VMs" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: No network interface misses what this question tests. VMs are full guests with virtual hardware including vNICs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "No network interface" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Virtualization eliminates physical networks — VMs/containers still need virtual and physical switching/routing underneath

questions.question: True or False: VRF and VLAN provide the same type of isolation.
questions.explanation: False — VLAN = L2 broadcast domain; VRF = L3 routing table isolation.

correct.explanation: False — VLAN = L2 broadcast domain; VRF = L3 routing table isolation.

incorrect.explanation: This statement is True, but the tested fact is: False — VLAN = L2 broadcast domain; VRF = L3 routing table isolation.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Equating VRF with VLAN — VRF isolates L3 routing; VLAN isolates L2 broadcast domains

questions.question: Docker is commonly associated with…
questions.explanation: Docker packages apps into containers sharing the host kernel.

correct.explanation: Docker packages apps into containers sharing the host kernel.

incorrect.explanation: Type 1 hypervisors misses what this question tests. Docker packages apps into containers sharing the host kernel.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Type 1 hypervisors" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: WPA3 misses what this question tests. Docker packages apps into containers sharing the host kernel.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "WPA3" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Spanning Tree is the wrong device role here. STP prevents Layer 2 loops — it does not provide inter-VLAN routing, MAC learning rules, or replace trunk configuration.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Choosing STP when the question asks for forwarding, routing, or VLAN data plane behavior

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Saying containers include a full guest OS — Containers share the host kernel — only apps/libs are packaged
